text.title: Prompt Engineering — Evaluation and Debugging — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the prompt-engineering-evaluation methodology. Sources: Zheng et al. — MT-Bench / LLM-as-judge (arXiv:2306.05685); Anthropic — evaluating prompts guide; OpenAI Evals framework.

rule.statement: Maintain a frozen evaluation suite per prompt: ≥ 50 inputs with expected behaviour, never edited without bumping suite version.
rule.rationale: An eval suite that drifts with the prompt cannot measure regression; freezing the suite is what makes scores comparable across iterations.

rule.statement: Run deterministic metrics (exact-match, regex, schema-pass) before LLM-as-judge; the LLM-as-judge tier is for the residual.
rule.rationale: Deterministic metrics are free and reproducible; LLM-as-judge is expensive and variable. Stack them, do not skip the first.

rule.statement: When using LLM-as-judge, fix temperature=0 and pin the judge model version; report the judge id+version with every score.
rule.rationale: LLM-as-judge scores at temperature > 0 are noise; pinned model+temperature is the only way to get reproducible numbers.

rule.statement: Wire eval into CI: prompt PRs that drop any tracked metric > 5% on the frozen suite block merge without an explicit override.
rule.rationale: Without a hard gate, regressions ship in 'minor wording' PRs and are detected weeks later by customer reports.

rule.statement: For revenue-critical prompts, ensemble 2+ judge models from different families; report inter-judge agreement.
rule.rationale: Single-judge scores are biased toward outputs that look like the judge's family; multi-judge ensembles correct the family bias.
